card.intuition: Bad initialization breaks training: zero weights make all neurons identical (symmetry problem); too-large weights cause exploding activations; too-small cause vanishing gradients. Xavier init scales by 1/√(fanᵢₙ + fanₒᵤₜ) — keeps variance constant for tanh/sigmoid. He init scales by √(2/fanᵢₙ) — accounts for ReLU zeroing half its inputs.
card.analogy: Tuning a radio before broadcasting: start too loud and you clip the signal (exploding); start too quiet and you lose the signal in noise (vanishing). Xavier/He initialization finds the right initial volume so the signal propagates cleanly through all layers.
card.time_complexity: O(Σ dₗ · dₗ₊₁) — one-time cost at startup
card.space_complexity: O(Σ dₗ · dₗ₊₁) for weight matrices
when_to_use: He init: ReLU and variants (Leaky ReLU, ELU) — accounts for half-zeroed activations
Orthogonal init: RNNs — preserves gradient norms across time steps
gotchas: Never initialise all weights to zero — symmetry means all neurons learn the same thing
Bias can safely start at zero (or small constant) — only weights need careful init
With batch normalisation, exact init matters less, but still helps convergence speed

exercise.prompt: Implement Xavier and He weight initialization.
exercise.starter_code:
import random, math

def xavier_uniform(fan_in, fan_out):
    """
    Xavier uniform init: U(-limit, limit) where limit = sqrt(6 / (fan_in + fan_out)).
    Returns a fan_in x fan_out weight matrix.
    """
    limit = math.sqrt(6.0 / (fan_in + fan_out))
    return [[random.uniform(-limit, limit) for _ in range(fan_out)]
            for _ in range(fan_in)]

def he_normal(fan_in, fan_out):
    """
    He normal init: N(0, std) where std = sqrt(2 / fan_in).
    Returns a fan_in x fan_out weight matrix.
    """
    std = ___________
    return [[random.gauss(0, std) for _ in range(fan_out)]
            for _ in range(fan_in)]
hints: He init std = sqrt(2 / fan_in). The factor 2 compensates for ReLU discarding ~half the activations.
exercise.test_code:
import math, random
random.seed(42)

# Xavier: values in [-limit, limit]
W_x = xavier_uniform(128, 64)
limit = math.sqrt(6.0 / (128 + 64))
assert all(-limit <= W_x[i][j] <= limit for i in range(128) for j in range(64))

# He: check std approx sqrt(2/fan_in)
W_h = he_normal(128, 64)
vals = [W_h[i][j] for i in range(128) for j in range(64)]
mean = sum(vals)/len(vals)
var  = sum((v-mean)**2 for v in vals)/len(vals)
expected_std = math.sqrt(2.0/128)
assert abs(math.sqrt(var) - expected_std) < 0.02, f'std mismatch: {math.sqrt(var):.4f} vs {expected_std:.4f}'
assert len(W_h) == 128 and len(W_h[0]) == 64
print('PASS')
